original: Donus begins his reign as Catholic Pope replacing Pope Adeodatus II
Peace treaty between the Chinese Jin and Song dynasties, the Song resume paying tribute after two years of fighting
King Alfonso IV of Aragon crowned
English invasion army under King Edward lands at Calais
Utrecht conquers Ijsselstein
Second Battle of Breitenfeld, aka First Battle of Leipzig; victory for the Swedish army under Field Marshal Lennart Torstenson over Holy Roman Empire army under Archduke Leopold Wilhelm of Austria
12,000 Jews massacred by Chmielnicki hordes in Narol Podlia

King Philip's War: Plymouth Colony Governor Josiah Winslow leads Plymouth, Rhode Island, Massachusetts Bay and Connecticut militias attacks against the Narragansetts, fearing they would join King Philip's cause
Scottish settlers make landfall in Panama, establishing the ill-fated 'Darien Venture' colony
Suriname government gives French hijacker Jacques Cassard, ƒ682,800
Willem IV Prince of Orange (11) appointed Stadtholder Gelderland
English Ohio Trade Company forms first trading post
Boston: anti-English Committee of Correspondence forms
Treaty of Fulda signed in Germany after Battle of Leipzig
Akbar Khan successfully revolts against Shah Shuja in Afghanistan
Cobblestone paving of Washington St between Dupont and Kearny starts
American Civil War: Western Department Union General John C. Frémont is relieved of command and replaced by David Hunter
Women's fashion magazine "Harper's Bazaar" is 1st published
Time zone: New Zealand officially adopts a standard time to be observed nationally
Verney Cameron reaches Benguela in Angola from Africa's east coast, the first European to cross equatorial Africa from sea to sea
Dutch New-Malthusiaanse Union forms
German patent granted to inventor Carl Benz for "vehicle with gas engine operation", the first automobile (No. DE37435C)
North Dakota becomes 39th & South Dakota becomes 40th state in the United States
French poet Paul Verlaine visits Netherlands
First organised auto race in the United States is run in Chicago; 6 cars race over 52-mile lakefront course; 2 finish because of blizzard conditions
French government of Dupuy forms
Boers begin siege of Ladysmith, Natal
British newspaper "Daily Mirror" begins publishing
Lyceum Theater (New Lyceum) opens at 149 W 45th St NYC
New Amsterdam Theater opens at 214 W 42nd St NYC
Ottawa Mint Proclamation is published
St. Louis Browns player-manager George Stovall, fired the previous summer, is the first MLB player to jump to the Federal League, signing to manage the Kansas City Packers
Great Britain annexes Cyprus
Great Britain declares the entire North Sea a military area: neutral ships will transit it at their own risk
Russia declares war on the Ottoman Empire
First US election by proportional representation, Ashtabula, Ohio
Battle of Verdun: Fort Vaux reconquered from Germans by French troops without firing a shot
In WWI the 1st US soldiers are killed in combat: James Gresham, Thomas Enright and Merle Hay
Lansing-Ishii Agreement; US recognizes Japan's privileges in China
Westinghouse radio station in East Pittsburgh, KDKA begins broadcasting, with presidential and local election returns
Allies deliberate over German mark
Bloody street fights in Aachen led to establishment of the ill-fated Rhenish Republic
Sunday Express publishes first British crossword puzzle
Warren, Dixon and Young's musical "Laugh Parade" premieres in NYC
The "Great Emu War" begins: Australian soldiers armed with Lewis Guns seek to cull the Emu population over crop destruction in Campion district, Western Australia
At 3pm the BBC begins the world's first regular high-definition TV broadcast service from specially constructed studios at Alexandra Palace, North London
The Canadian Broadcasting Corporation (CBC) is established
11th day of battle at El Alamein: British assault on Tel el Aqqaqir
Jewish ghetto of Riga in Latvia is destroyed
Canadian troops occupy Knokke in Belgium
US 28th Infantry division opens assault on Schmidt Hurtgenwald
New York City's WABC (AM & FM) radio stations change call letters to WCBS
New York City's WEAF (AM & FM) radio stations change call letters to WNBC

After just 6 games of the season, Chicago Blackhawks trade NHL scoring leader Max Bentley along with Cy Thomas to Toronto Maple Leafs for Gus Bodnar, Bud Poile, Gaye Stewart, Bob Goldham, and Ernie Dickens
WJZ TV channel 13 in Baltimore, MD (ABC) begins broadcasting
Netherlands ends rationing programs started during WWII for distribution of textiles, meat, and cheese
The Netherlands recognizes its former colony Indonesia as a sovereign state at conclusion of the Round Table Conference at the Hague, Netherlands
The Clover Dairy Company test-market the first concentrated milk (Sealtest) in the U.S. in Wilmington, Delaware
Pakistan becomes an Islamic republic
Charles Diggs Jr is elected Michigan's 1st black congressman to the House of Representatives
Hungary appeals for UN assistance against Soviet invasion
1st titanium mill opened at Toronto, Ohio
The Levelland UFO Case in Levelland, Texas, generates national publicity, remains one of the most impressive UFO cases in American history
Los Angeles Rams beat Chicago Bears, 41-35 before 90,833 fans at the Los Angeles Coliseum; NFL single-game attendance record
Charles Van Doren confesses during a congressional investigation that TV quiz show "Twenty-One" was fixed
The first section of the M1 motorway, the first inter-urban motorway in the United Kingdom, is opened between the present junctions 5 and 18, along with the M10 motorway and M45 motorway
Dmitri Shostakovich's 8th String quartet premieres in Leningrad
Max Frisch's  play "Andorra" premieres in Zürich
Columbia Broadcasting System buys 80% share in the New York Yankees Baseball Club for $11.2 million; later purchases club outright
KHSD TV channel 11 in Lead, SD (ABC) begins broadcasting
The Cuban Adjustment Act comes into force, allowing 123,000 Cubans opportunity to apply for permanent residence in the US

A banned march in Derry, North Ireland, by members of the Derry Citizen's Action Committee (DCAC) is joined by thousands; due to the number of people taking part, the Royal Ulster Constabulary is unable to prevent it

Quarterbacks Billy Kilmer of New Orleans and St Louis' Charlie Johnson each pass for 6 touchdowns for combined NFL record of 12 passing TDs in a game; Saints beat Cardinals, 51-42 at Busch Memorial Stadium
Baltimore Orioles pitcher Pat Dobson no-hits the Yomiuri Giants, 2 - 0; first no-hitter in history of exhibition play between American and Japanese teams
Construction begins on Kingdome, Seattle
Government of the Republic of Ireland introduce a bill to remove the special position of the Catholic Church from the Irish Constitution
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
OLADE (Latin American Energy Organization) forms
78 die when the Time Go-Go Club in Seoul, South Korea burns down. Six of the victims jumped to their deaths from the seventh floor after a club official barred the doors after the fire started.
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
First sports event at The Summit arena in Houston - NBA's Houston Rockets beat Milwaukee Bucks, 104-89; future Rockets coach Rudy Tomjanovich top scores with 24 points
Microbiologist Carl R. Woese and scientists from the University of Illinois announce the identification of methanogens, a form of microbial life (Archaea) dating back some 3.5 billion years
Philadelphia Phillies pitcherSteve Carltonwins his second Cy Young Award, outpointing Tommy John of the LA Dodgers; Carlton leads National League with 23 wins and posts 2.64 ERA
Crew of Soyuz 29 returns to Earth aboard Soyuz 31
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Studio 54's owners are arrested for tax evasion
Fire in Salung tunnel, Afghanistan, 1,000+ Russians die
Archbishop Hickey conducts papal investigation of Archbishop Hunthausen of Seattle
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll in the Pacific
Velma Barfield becomes the first woman executed in the United States since 1962
LA Kings score 5 goals in a span of 5:37, the fastest in NHL team history; rout Hartford Whalers, 8-1 at Hartford Civic Center
Tulsa's Gordon Brown (214) and Steve Gage (206) set NCAA football record for combined rushing yards in one game for 2 teammates who gained more than 200 yards each in 42-26 win at Wichita State
Entertainer Lola Falona is diagnosed with multiple schlerosis
The Morris worm, the first internet-distributed computer worm to gain mainstream media attention is launched from MIT, strikes Pentagon, SDI research lab and six universities
First NBA game at Target Center, Minneapolis, Minnesota; Rolando Blackman top scores with 21 points as Timberwolves beat Mavericks, 98-85
First regular season game played outside North America by any major professional sports league the NBA's Phoenix Suns beat the Utah Jazz, 119-96 at the Tokyo Metropolitan Gymnasium in Japan
Golden State Warriors register a 162-158 win over the Denver Nuggets at McNichols Arena, with the 320 total points setting an NBA record for most points scored by 2 teams in a non-overtime game
Bartholomew I becomes the Patriarch of Constantinople, head of 300 million followers of the Eastern Orthodox Church
Jermaine Jackson releases "Word to the Badd!!" anti Michael song
Nevada makes biggest comeback in NCAA football history, overcoming a 35 point 3rd quarter deficit to rally and beat Weber State, 55-49
First test flight of Airbus A330
Christie Todd Whitman (R) elected 1st woman governor of New Jersey
Dow Jones Industrial Average hits record 3697.64
Ehud Olmert elected mayor of Jerusalem
Benzine explosion in Dronka Egypt, 400+ killed
NFL announces the expansion Carolina Panthers would play in the NFC West, while the Jacksonville Jaguars is assigned to the AFC Central
"Busker Alley" musical premieres at St James Theatre, NY
Spanish Broadcasting System buys NY radio station WPAT-FM for $83.5M
Miami Heat’s Pat Riley becomes eighth coach to win 800 games in 97-95 win at Indiana; milestone accomplished 2 weeks shy of his 15th coaching anniversary, faster than any coach in NBA history

NFL  San Diego Chargers Eric Metcalf returns punts for touchdowns of 85 & 67 yards in 38-31 loss at Cincinnati; joins Hall of Famer Jack Christiansen as only players in NFL history to return punts for TDs twice in a game
Typhoon Linda kills at least 208 in southern Vietnam
The first crew arrives at the International Space Station
New York City Marathon: Margaret Okayo of Kenya wins in 2:22:31 to break her own record; her countryman Martin Lel wins men's race in 2:10:30
Philip Glass' Symphony No. 8, premieres with the Bruckner Orchester Linz conducted by Dennis Russell Davies, at the Brooklyn Academy of Music in Brooklyn, NYC
New York City Marathon: cancelled because of the effects of Hurricane Sandy in NYC the week before
Masahiro Tanaka's record streak of 30 consecutive starts without a loss ends when the Rakuten Golden Eagles' ace is beaten, 4-2, by the Yomiuri Giants in Game 6 of the Japan Baseball Series
60 people were killed and 110 injured by a suicide bombing in Lahore, Pakistan
New York City Marathon: Wilson Kipsang of Kenya wins in 2:10:59; compatriot Mary Keitany wins first of 3 straight women's titles in 2:25:07
Baseball World Series: Chicago Cubs beat Cleveland Indians, 8-7 in Game 7 at Progressive Field, Cleveland; first Cubs' title in 108 years; MVP: Chicago utility Ben Zobrist
Tiger thought to have killed 13 people shot dead after month-long hunt near Pandharkawada, central India
9th Rugby World Cup, Yokohama: South African fly-half Handré Pollard lands 6 penalties and 2 conversions as Springboks beat favourites England, 32-12 for their 3rd title
Baby Shark by Pinkfong becomes the most-watched video on YouTube with over 7.04 billion views
Gunmen storm Kabul University, Afghanistan, shooting at least 22 dead with Islamic State group claiming responsibility
Former police captains Eric Adams elected the second African American mayor of New York
Jihadist gunman ambush and kill 69 people, including a local mayor in south-west Niger, adding to the 530 killed in 2021 to date
Ethiopia’s government and Tigrayan authorities reach an agreement end fighting after two years at peace talks in South Africa
US Federal Reserve hikes interest rates for a fourth straight time to range of between 3.75% and 4.00%, its highest since 2008 in an effort to fight ongoing inflation
Australian Erin Patterson charged with three counts of murder and five of attempted murder through mushroom poisoning in high profile case
Former cryptocurrency star Sam Bankman-Fried convicted of seven counts of fraud and conspiracy involving his companies FTX and Alameda Research in a New York court
Russia carries out its most extensive artillery attack of the year in Ukraine as Ukraine’s commander in chief admits the war has reached a deadlock
Storm Ciarán batters Northern Europe and Southern England with hurricane force winds, leaving 1.2 million in France without power and at least four dead
Kemi Badenoch becomes the first black woman to lead a major British political party, when elected leader of the Conservative Party
GeneralGeorge Washington, later 1st US President, bids farewell to his army after the American Revolutionary War
DemocratFranklin Pierceelected 14th President of the United States
American abolitionistJohn Brownfound guilty of murder, inciting slaves to revolt, and treason against the Virginia Territory during his raid of Harpers Ferry Armory, and sentenced to hang
James A. Garfield(R) elected 20th President of the United States
Austro-Hungarian Jewish activistTheodor Herzlarrives in Jerusalem
US bankerJ. P. Morganlocks over 40 bankers in his library to force them to find ways to avert New York banking crisis
British government proclaims support for a Jewish homeland in Palestine in theBalfour Declaration
Warren G. Hardingis elected President of the United States, defeating Democrat candidate James M. Cox
Eugene O'Neill's play "Anna Christie" premieres in NYC
Gustav Stresemann's German coalition Government collapses after Social Democrat ministers step down
Dmitri Shostakovich's 1st Symphony premieres in US with Leopold Stokowski leading the Philadelphia Orchestra at The Academy of Music, Philadelpha
Coronation of Ras Tafari Makonnen asHaile SelassieI, the 225th Emperor of the Ethiopian Solomonic Dynasty
MLB All-Star team led byConnie Mackand includingBabe Ruth,Jimmie FoxxandLou Gehrigstarts 18-game tour against Japanese Big-Six University League
Boston Red Sox first basemanJimmie Foxxis voted Most Valuable Player of the American League for the third time
Howard Hughesflies "Spruce Goose", a huge wooden airplane for the first and last time
US PresidentHarry Trumanis re-elected in an upset victory over Republican candidateThomas E. Dewey
BBC radio comedy "Hancock's Half Hour" debuts starringTony Hancockand Sid James, written by Ray Galton and Alan Simpson
Strom Thurmondis the 1st senator elected by write-in vote (South Carolina)
David Ben-Gurionforms Israeli government
New York Yankees outfielderRoger Marisbeats teammateMickey Mantlefor American League MVP Award, 225-222; second-closest vote ever
Penguin Books cleared of obscenity in UK for publishingD. H. Lawrence's "Lady Chatterley's Lover"
Ngô Đình Diệm, the President of South Vietnam, is overthrown and assassinated in a coup by the South Vietnamese Army
Faisalsucceeds Saud as king of Saudi Arabia
Craig Breedlovedriving FIA-legal four-wheeler, Sonic I, breaks land speed record with a two-run average of 555.483 mph (893.963 km/h) at the Bonneville Salt Flats, Utah
41-year old Detroit right wingGordie Howepicks up his 19th and final NHL hat trick as the Red Wings beat Pittsburgh Penguins, 4-3; Howe is the oldest player to score an NHL hat-trick
Retired boxerJersey Joe WalcottKO's William Strang in election for Sheriff of Camden County, New Jersey; first African-American to be elected to the position
Philadelphia Phillies pitcherSteve Carltonis unanimously selected National League Cy Young Award winner; wins 27 games for Phillies, who as a team win only 59 games
"Barbra Streisand...and Other Musical Instruments" special premieres on CBS TV
Apple Records releases "Ringo",Ringo Starr's third studio album, in the US; his biggest commercial and critical success, featuring the singles "Photograph", "You're Sixteen", and "Oh My My"
Atlanta Braves trade then MLB home run kingHank Aaronto Milwaukee Brewers for outfielder Dave May
Democrat candidateJimmy Carteris elected President of the United States, defeating incumbent Republican PresidentGerald Ford;Walter Mondaleelected vice president
Arnold Shapiro's TV documentary "Scared Straight", narrated byPeter Falkas a controversial and questionable deterrent to juvenile delinquency, debuts on Los Angeles' KTLA-TV

Wayne Gretzkyis sold to the Edmonton Oilers after just 8 games with the Indianapolis Racers of the WHA (scoring 6 points); scores 104 points in 72 games (both teams combined) and is named WHA rookie of the year
Peter Shaffer's musical "Amadeus" premieres in London
"Thriller" single released worldwide byMichael Jackson
US PresidentRonald Reagansigns bill establishing DrMartin Luther King Jr.holiday

German tennis starBoris Beckerwins his third tournament in 3 weeks in different continents; beats Sergio Casal of Spain 6-4, 6-3, 7-6 in the final of the Paris Open after victories in Japan and Australia
George Harrisonreleases album "Cloud 9" &Paul McCartneyreleases greatest hits album "All the Best"
A's shortstop Walt Weiss wins AL rookie of year; 3rd straight for Oakland; Jose Canseco 1986,Mark McGwire1987
Mexican radio station erroneously reportsMike Tysonhad died in car crash
"BlackadderGoes Forth" final episode "Goodbyeee" airs on BBC-TV starringRowan Atkinsonand Tony Robinson, written by Richard Curtis and Ben Elton
2nd Rugby World Cup Final,Twickenham: Australia beats England, 12-6 with Wallabies fly-half Michael Lynagh landing 2 penalties and a conversion
ActorRoger Moore(James Bond) has his enlarged prostate removed
First commemorative bricks are laid atBob FellerMuseum, Van Meter, Iowa
Rudy Giulianiwins the New York mayoral election, becomes 1st Republican mayor since 1965
Rodgers & Hammerstein's television musical "Cinderella", starringBrandy Norwood,Whitney Houston, andBernadette Peterspremieres on ABC-TV

Mark Messierplays his 1,616th NHL game, a 3-2 Rangers loss against the St. Blues, to move into 2nd place on the all-time games played list; passes Larry Murphy and ends career with 1,756 games, just 11 shy of Gordie Howe
US sitcom "Arrested Development" Premieres created by Mitchell Hurwitz, starringJason Bateman, Porta de Rossi,Michael Ceraand an ensemble cast on Fox
George W. Bushis re-elected as President of the United States, defeating Democrat candidateJohn Kerry
7th Latin Grammy Awards:Shakirafirst female artist to win Record of the Year, Album of the Year, and Song of the Year
Patrick Kaneis named the NHL Rookie of the Month for October
British McLaren driverLewis Hamiltonfinishes 4th in season-ending Brazilian Grand Prix at Autódromo José Carlos Pace to clinch his first F1 World Drivers Championship by 1 point from Felipe Massa
Lancaster House agreements signed between British PMDavid Cameronand France'sNicolas Sarkozy: a 50 year armed forces defence cooperation treaty and a nuclear technology cooperation pact
Prodigious Portuguese strikerCristiano Ronaldoscores his 100th goal for Real Madrid in his 105th game for the club in a 2-0 Champions League win over Olympique Lyon
Brazilian surferGabriel Medinaclaims ASP World Junior Title by beating Ramzi Boukhiam of Morocco in a high-scoring final at Joaquina Beach in Florianopolis, Brazil
"The Hunger Games: Mockingjay – Part 2" directed by Francis Lawrence, starringJennifer Lawrence,Josh HutchersonandLiam Hemsworthpremieres in Berlin
Prime MinisterMalcolm Turnbullscraps Knights and Dames from Australia's honours system
Jerome Powell nominated by US PresidentDonald Trumpto be next Chair of the Federal Reserve
On This Day andXiaomilaunch a content partnership to display today in history content in Xiaomi's calendar app to users in India

Johnny Depploses libel case against UK newspaper "The Sun", which called him a "wife-beater", said he assaulted wifeAmber Heard

Apple Records release the last Beatles song "Now and Then" as a double A-sided single, backed with their first "From Me To You"; the new song was built from an unfinished 1978John Lennondemo and 1995 attempt to complete it that had been abandoned due to technical issues, resolved by new technology
